assure.description: Trust-surface coverage posture: how many declared trust boundaries got a definite verdict vs. how many are honestly unknown, plus waiver debt. Identical to the CLI `assure` JSON.

baselined_total.description: Findings suppressed by the baseline in this scan.

boundaries_total.description: Denominator: count of anchored (trust-declared) entities. proven + defect_total + len(unknown) == boundaries_total.

coverage_pct.description: Share of known boundaries with a definite verdict over known boundaries plus unanalyzed files, rounded to 1 decimal. null when there are no boundaries and no unanalyzed files.

defect_total.description: Boundaries with a definite defect verdict (a defect counts as COVERED — the engine reached a verdict).

engine_limited.description: Under-scan pressure: entity-level unknowns with an engine reason plus unanalyzed files.

judged_total.description: Findings suppressed by judge verdicts in this scan.

proven.description: Boundaries with a definite clean verdict.

unanalyzed_rule_ids.description: Distinct under-scan rule ids present in the scan findings, sorted lexicographically.

unanalyzed_total.description: Files discovered but never analyzed. Each counts as at least one uncovered surface item in coverage_pct.

unknown.description: The honesty gap: anchored entities whose trust could not be proven either way, sorted by qualname.

location.description: Where the entity is declared; both fields null when no location is known.

qualname.description: Qualified name of the anchored entity.

reason.description: Engine under-scan FACT message when the body was not analysed (parse/recursion skip), else null (undeclared / unprovable).

tier.description: Declared trust tier, or null if undeclared.

waiver_debt.description: Configured waivers with days-to-expiry, sorted by fingerprint. Populated even when nothing was analysable.

days_left.description: Days until expiry; may be NEGATIVE for a lapsed waiver; null for no expiry.

expires.description: ISO date the waiver expires, or null for no expiry.

attest.description: Signed, reproducible evidence bundle: a deterministic payload plus an HMAC-SHA256 signature under the shared project key (tamper-evidence within the key-holding trust domain, not asymmetric proof).

payload.description: The signed, deterministic attestation payload (canonical compact key-sorted JSON is the reproducibility target).

attested_at.description: ISO date the bundle was built; re-derivation on verify uses this as `today`.

boundaries.description: Per-boundary trust verdicts, sorted by qualname; empty when nothing was analysable.

qualname.description: Qualified name of the anchored entity.

sei.description: Loomweave SEI resolved at build time; null without a Loomweave client or when unresolvable.

tier.description: Declared trust tier, or null if undeclared.

verdict.description: The entity's trust verdict from the single source of truth (classify_entity_trust).

commit.description: `git rev-parse HEAD` of the tree, or null for a non-git tree / missing git.

dirty.description: True iff the working tree had uncommitted changes (MCP refuses dirty trees unless allow_dirty=true).

posture.description: The trust-surface coverage posture at attestation time (same shape as the `assure` tool result).

coverage_pct.description: null when there are no boundaries and no unanalyzed files.

unknown.description: Anchored entities with no definite verdict, sorted by qualname.

waiver_debt.description: Configured waivers with days-to-expiry (the only date-sensitive payload field), sorted by fingerprint.

ruleset_hash.description: Deterministic 'sha256:<hex>' over the effective scan policy.

sei_source.description: 'loomweave' iff a client was supplied AND at least one SEI resolved; else 'unavailable'.

wardline_version.description: Wardline version that produced the bundle.

schema.description: Wire-contract tag; bound into the HMAC so a relabel cannot verify.

signature.description: HMAC-SHA256 over the canonical {schema, payload} envelope bytes.

key_id.description: Non-secret 8-hex short id of the signing key (distinguishes keys without revealing them).

baseline.description: Success payload of the wardline MCP `baseline` tool: records the result of generating (or finding an existing) suppression baseline for the project.

already_exists.description: Only present when overwrite was not requested: true if a baseline file already existed (nothing was written; counts reflect the existing file), false if a new baseline was created. Absent when overwrite=true succeeds.

baselined_count.description: Number of finding fingerprints in the baseline. On a fresh generation this is the count just written; when the baseline already existed (already_exists=true) it is the count in the existing file.

path.description: Absolute path of the baseline file.

reason.description: Caller-supplied reason for creating the baseline; null when not provided.

decorator_coverage.description: Row-level inventory of every trust-decorated entity under the project (the row-level sibling of assure): each declared trust-surface entity with its current verdict, findings, identity binding, and open-work state, plus a rollup summary.

rows.description: One row per declared trust-decorated entity, sorted by qualname. Empty when the scan produced no analysis context.

active_finding_fingerprints.description: Sorted fingerprints of active (non-suppressed) defect findings on the entity.

actual_tier.description: Engine-computed actual return taint; null when not computed.

declared_tier.description: Declared return trust tier; null when undeclared.

decorators.description: Decorators as declared, each prefixed with '@'.

finding_state.description: defect when active findings exist; suppressed when only accepted findings exist; unknown when the verdict is unknown; else clean.

identity.description: Cross-tool identity binding for the row. Degrades to available=false with a reason when Loomweave is not configured / unreachable / resolved no SEI.

available.description: True only when an SEI was resolved for the entity.

content_hash.description: Current content hash from the binding, when available.

content_status.description: Content axis: has the entity's code changed?

identity_status.description: Identity axis: is this the same entity?

reason.description: Why identity is unavailable (e.g. 'loomweave not configured', 'no SEI resolved'); null when an SEI was resolved.

sei.description: Opaque stable entity identifier; null when not resolved.

line.description: Entity start line; null when the declared qualname has no scanned entity.

path.description: Source file path; null when the declared qualname has no scanned entity.

qualname.description: The entity's qualified name, minted relative to the scan root.

suppressed_finding_fingerprints.description: Sorted fingerprints of accepted (baselined/waived/judged) defect findings.

verdict.description: Three-valued, fail-closed trust verdict for the entity.

work.description: Open work from Filigree, keyed on the SEI. available=false with a reason when Filigree is not configured / unreachable / there is no SEI binding.

content_status.description: stale when any ticket binding drifted; unknown when a compare was impossible.

reason.description: Why the section is unavailable; null when available.

tickets.description: Filigree issues bound to the entity.

drift.description: True when the issue was bound to a PRIOR version of the entity (content hash at attach no longer matches).

summary.description: Rollup counts over rows by finding_state.

doctor.description: Doctor success payload: the CLI `doctor --fix` machine-readable envelope (install/federation health checks) plus the running MCP server's self-identification block and a `server.freshness` check appended to the check list.

checks.description: Uniform health-check verdicts: wardline.config, mcp.registration, marker_package, loomweave.url, filigree.url, decorator_grammar, scan.output_path, auth.token, filigree.auth, optionally doctor.filigree_url for rejected MCP caller input, then server.freshness last.

fixed.description: True when this run's repair (repair: true) corrected the underlying condition.

message.description: Human/agent-readable detail. Present only when the check produced a non-empty message (always on errors; sometimes on informational ok results).

next_actions.description: One '<check id>: <message>' action line per failed check that carries a message; empty when everything is healthy.

ok.description: True only when every check (including the appended server.freshness check) passed.

server.description: The running MCP server's self-identification: detects a stale long-lived server (source on disk newer than process start).

fresh.description: False when on-disk package source changed after this process started — the server is serving OLD code and must be restarted.

project_root.description: Absolute project root this server is confined to.

source_latest_mtime.description: ISO-8601 UTC mtime of the newest *.py file under the imported wardline package, or null when nothing was statable.

source_latest_path.description: Package-relative path of that newest source file, or null.

started_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of server process start.

dossier.description: One-call entity dossier envelope: Wardline's own trust posture (always re-derived fresh) plus Loomweave linkages and Filigree open work, each cross-tool section degrading to an honest unavailable shape (available=false + reason) when its source is absent. Token-bounded with an explicit truncation marker.

identity.description: Who the entity is plus its two-axis freshness (identity axis / content axis). Never trimmed by the budgeter.

content_hash.description: Current content hash from the binding, when available.

content_status.description: Content axis: has the entity's code changed? Never inferred from identity.

identity_status.description: Identity axis: is this the same entity? Never inferred from content.

keyed_on_sei.description: True when the cross-tool sections were keyed on the SEI rather than a locator.

kind.description: Entity kind (e.g. function); null when unknown.

path.description: Source file path of the entity.

qualname.description: The entity's qualified name, minted relative to the scan root.

sei.description: Opaque stable entity identifier (the cross-tool binding key); null when no Loomweave binding was resolved.

linkages.description: Call-graph neighbourhood from Loomweave. available=false with a reason when Loomweave is not configured / unreachable / serves no HTTP linkages.

callees.description: Callee entity locators; empty when unavailable. May be trimmed by the token budgeter.

callers.description: Caller entity locators; empty when unavailable. May be trimmed by the token budgeter.

reason.description: Why the section is unavailable or degraded (e.g. one-sided linkage failure); null when fully available.

scc_peers.description: Strongly-connected-component peers (currently always empty: SCC membership is not served over HTTP).

shape.description: Signature and decorators as declared in source.

decorators.description: Decorators as declared, each prefixed with '@'.

synthesis.description: Best-effort one-paragraph actionable join of all sections; null when dropped to fit the token budget.

truncation.description: Elision-honest truncation marker: truncated=false on a complete envelope; when true, elided names every trimmed list with shown-of-total counts.

section.description: Dotted list path that was trimmed, e.g. "linkages.callers" or "trust.active_findings".

note.description: Human-readable trim summary; null when not truncated.

trust.description: Wardline's OWN trust posture, re-derived from a live scan (fresh by construction).

active_findings.description: Active (non-suppressed) defect findings on the entity. May be trimmed by the token budgeter (see truncation.elided).

actual_return.description: Engine-computed actual return taint; null when not computed.

declared_return.description: Declared return trust tier; null when undeclared.

freshness.description: Constant: the trust section is re-derived on demand, never stale.

gate_verdict.description: Three-valued, fail-closed verdict: defect (active findings), clean (declared posture that conforms), unknown (undeclared/unprovable/under-scanned).

suppressed_findings.description: Count of accepted (baselined/waived/judged) defects — known debt a clean verdict must not hide.

unanalyzed_reason.description: Engine under-scan fact (parse error / recursion skip) when the body was not analysed; else null.

work.description: Open work from Filigree, keyed on the SEI. available=false with a reason when Filigree is not configured / unreachable / there is no binding.

content_status.description: stale when any ticket binding drifted; unknown when a compare was impossible.

reason.description: Why the section is unavailable; null when available.

tickets.description: Filigree issues bound to the entity. May be trimmed by the token budgeter.

drift.description: True when the issue was bound to a PRIOR version of the entity (content hash at attach no longer matches).

explain_taint.description: Success payload of the explain_taint tool: the taint provenance slice for one finding (single source: core/explain.explain_taint_result, shared with the CLI `wardline explain-taint`). Served either from a fresh Loomweave store fact (no re-scan) or from an SP8 re-run; both paths produce this same key set. With chain=true and a configured Loomweave store, a `chain` block is additionally attached.

chain.description: Full N-hop taint chain from the sink to the originating boundary, walked from the Loomweave store. Present whenever the call passed chain=true: status 'walked' carries the hops; status 'unavailable' is the explicit C-10(c) degrade marker (no Loomweave store configured, or no sink qualname to anchor on) naming the missing capability and its enablement path — the walk never degrades silently.

enablement.description: unavailable only: how to enable the missing capability; null when walked.

hops.description: Ordered hops from the sink toward the boundary leaf. The walk stops cleanly at a boundary (contributing_callee_qualname null on the last hop) or truncates explicitly (see truncated_at).

contributing_callee_qualname.description: Next hop toward the boundary; null at the boundary leaf (clean finish).

qualname.description: Qualified name of the function at this hop.

tier_in.description: Actual trust tier arriving at this hop (from the stored fact; null when absent).

tier_out.description: Trust tier this hop declares it returns (from the stored fact; null when absent).

missing_capability.description: unavailable only: what the walk lacked ('loomweave_taint_store' or 'sink_qualname'); null when walked.

status.description: walked: the store walk ran (hops below). unavailable: the walk could not run; see missing_capability/enablement.

truncated_at.description: Qualified name of the next hop the walk could NOT take (stale/absent fact, read error, cycle, or max_hops reached) — truncation is always explicit; null means the chain reached the boundary cleanly.

fingerprint.description: Stable finding fingerprint. May be the empty string on the store-served path when the entity blob carries no per-finding rows (the entity is known, the specific finding is not).

immediate_tainted_callee.description: Bare trailing name of the call that introduced the untrusted return into the sink (null when unresolved).

location.description: Source location of the finding. path may be empty and line null on the store-served path when the blob has no per-finding rows.

line.description: 1-based start line of the finding (null when unknown).

path.description: Root-relative posix path of the finding's file (empty string when unknown on the store-served path).

remediation.description: Advisory fix-at-the-boundary hint derived from the explanation; never replaces the factual taint fields above.

caveat.description: Standing warning against blind decorator insertion / over-trusting the hint.

kind.description: boundary_placement for PY-WL-101 (place/repair a @trust_boundary at the validating function); sink_hygiene for the dangerous-sink family (rule-specific fix guidance naming the source and sink); review_required for rules with no automated hint.

rule_id.description: Rule the hint applies to (echoes the top-level rule_id).

sink_qualname.description: Sink the hint refers to (null when the finding has no qualname).

source_qualname.description: Taint source the hint refers to: the resolved boundary, else the immediate tainted callee, else null when unresolved.

resolved_call_count.description: Number of calls inside the sink the engine resolved during taint computation.

rule_id.description: Rule that produced the finding (e.g. PY-WL-101). May be the empty string on the store-served path when no per-finding row matched.

sink_qualname.description: Qualified name of the sink function the tainted value reaches (null when the engine has no qualname for the finding).

source_boundary_qualname.description: Originating boundary resolved one hop from the sink: qualified name of the boundary function the taint came from (null when not resolvable in one hop). On the store-served path this is the blob's contributing_callee_qualname.

source_resolution.description: C-10(c) honesty block: whether the taint source is named above, and when it is NOT, why and what capability would resolve it further — an explicit degrade marker, never nulls that read as a complete-but-empty answer.

enablement.description: unresolved only: how to enable the missing capability; null otherwise.

missing_capability.description: unresolved only: capability that could resolve further — 'loomweave_taint_store' when no store is configured; null when resolved or when nothing more would help.

reason.description: unresolved only: why wardline's own single-scan analysis could not name the source; null when resolved.

status.description: resolved when immediate_tainted_callee or source_boundary_qualname is named; unresolved otherwise.

tier_in.description: Actual (untrusted) trust tier arriving at the sink, e.g. EXTERNAL_RAW (null when the engine recorded none).

tier_out.description: Trust tier the sink declares it returns, e.g. INTEGRAL (null when the engine recorded none).

unresolved_call_count.description: Number of calls inside the sink the engine could NOT resolve (residual uncertainty in the explanation).

file_finding.description: Success payload of the file_finding tool: the outcome of promoting ONE finding (by fingerprint) into a tracked Filigree issue, fail-soft on reachability.

created.description: True when the promote created a NEW issue (vs returning an existing one).

disabled_reason.description: Why enrichment was unavailable (e.g. 'filigree unreachable', 'filigree 503'); null on success.

fingerprint.description: The fingerprint that was filed (echoed from the request).

identity_attach.description: Present only when attach_loomweave_identity=true was requested: the outcome of binding the finding's Loomweave entity identity to the filed issue.

attached.description: Whether the entity association was successfully attached to the Filigree issue.

attempted.description: Whether an identity attach was attempted at all (false when there is no issue_id or no Loomweave URL configured).

binding_kind.description: Whether the binding used a rename-stable SEI or a legacy locator; null when no binding was attempted.

content_hash.description: The entity content hash captured at attach time (drift-detection anchor); null when unresolved.

entity_id.description: The entity identifier used for the binding — a 'loomweave:eid:...' SEI or a legacy '{plugin}:function:{qualname}' locator.

reason.description: Human-readable reason when not attempted or skipped; null on success.

issue_id.description: The Filigree issue id the fingerprint was promoted into; null when unreachable or the fingerprint was not found.

not_found.description: True when Filigree was reachable but the fingerprint is unknown to it (404 — emit findings to Filigree first).

reachable.description: Whether Filigree's promote route was reachable. False on transport failure, 5xx outage, or 401/403 auth refusal (all soft).

fix.description: Success payload of the wardline MCP `fix` tool: mechanical autofix results (PY-WL-111 assert-at-boundary rewrites), either previewed (default / dry_run) or applied in-place (apply=true).

applied.description: True when fixes were written to disk (apply=true and not dry_run), false when this was a preview. Absent on the early-return path where the scan found no fixable findings.

items.description: Description of one fix in this file.

fixed.description: Open map of file path (relative to the scanned `path` argument, NOT the project root) -> list of human-readable descriptions of the fixes previewed/applied in that file. Empty object when no fixable findings were found or no fixes could be produced.

message.description: Human-readable summary, e.g. 'No fixable findings found.', 'Previewed fixes for N files.' or 'Applied fixes for N files.'

judge.description: Success payload of the judge tool: per-finding LLM triage verdicts plus the persistence outcome of FALSE_POSITIVE records.

held_back.description: FALSE_POSITIVE verdicts NOT persisted because their confidence fell below the write floor.

verdicts.description: One flattened verdict per triaged finding.

confidence.description: The judge's calibrated confidence in the verdict, 0.0 to 1.0.

fingerprint.description: Stable fingerprint of the judged finding.

label.description: The judge's verdict: a real defect vs an analyzer over-approximation.

path.description: Repo-relative file path of the finding.

rationale.description: The model's verbatim reasoning (the audit primitive); always a non-empty string.

wrote.description: FALSE_POSITIVE verdicts persisted to .wardline/judged.yaml (0 unless write=true).

rekey.description: Rekey success payload. Four shapes share the 'mode' discriminator: 'probe' (default, read-only dry run), 'apply' and 'resume' (journal block reporting the migration's per-leg state), and 'rollback' ({mode, restored, note}). All non-'mode' fields are mode-specific.

clean.description: probe only: true when there are no orphans and no collisions — an apply would carry every stored verdict (or, when no_op, there is simply nothing to migrate).

collisions.description: probe/apply/resume: ambiguous rekey mappings. Either multiple pre-rekey fingerprints collapse to one new fingerprint, or one pre-rekey fingerprint fans out to multiple new fingerprints. All involved old fingerprints are orphaned, not carried.

message.description: WLN-ENGINE-FINGERPRINT-COLLISION or WLN-ENGINE-FINGERPRINT-FANOUT diagnostic explaining that the verdicts are orphaned.

new_fp.description: Collapse: the new-scheme fingerprint that more than one old fingerprint maps onto. Fan-out: null, with new_fps carrying the candidate new fingerprints.

new_fps.description: Fan-out only: the candidate new-scheme fingerprints one old fingerprint maps onto. Omitted for collapse collisions.

old_fps.description: The ambiguous pre-rekey fingerprints (sorted). Fan-out entries contain one old fingerprint.

complete.description: apply/resume: true when every migration leg is done.

current_scheme_stores.description: probe only: store file names ALREADY at the live fingerprint scheme — a rekey is a no-op for them; their entries were matched against the current scan's fingerprints.

fingerprint_scheme_from.description: apply/resume: the fingerprint scheme being migrated from (e.g. 'wlfp1').

fingerprint_scheme_to.description: apply/resume: the fingerprint scheme being migrated to (e.g. 'wlfp2').

legs.description: apply/resume: per-leg migration state, in application order (baseline, judged, waivers, filigree).

carried_count.description: Number of stored verdicts re-keyed and carried forward by this leg (count only; can be the whole store).

debt.description: Filigree leg only: recorded reconciliation debt when the leg soft-failed (re-run with apply to retry); null otherwise.

done.description: True when this leg has been applied and persisted.

name.description: Leg name: one of 'baseline', 'judged', 'waivers', 'filigree' in a journal this code wrote.

orphaned_count.description: Number of stored verdicts this leg dropped — never silent; the full verbatim list is recorded in the on-disk migration journal.

orphaned_sample.description: Bounded sample of the old fingerprints this leg dropped (counts are authoritative; full list in the migration journal).

matched.description: probe only: count of distinct stored fingerprints that map to a current finding — each store is judged against ITS OWN scheme (a wlfp1 store via the migration remap, a store already at the live scheme via the current fingerprints).

mode.description: Which rekey operation ran. 'probe' is the read-only default; 'apply'/'resume'/'rollback' are explicit, mutually exclusive write modes.

next_action.description: apply/resume, only when the migration is INCOMPLETE: instruction to re-run the rekey tool to finish pending leg(s).

next_pending_leg.description: apply/resume: name of the first not-done leg, or null when the migration is complete.

no_op.description: probe only: true when every populated store already carries the live scheme — no fingerprint migration is pending and an apply would be refused.

note.description: rollback only: caveat that Filigree associations from the forward run are NOT reversed (no remap endpoint); reconcile manually if needed.

orphan_cause.description: probe/apply/resume: fixed explanation string for why a stored fingerprint can orphan (source moved/deleted, or a custom multi-emit rule not surfacing taint_path_v0).

orphaned_count.description: probe only: number of stored OLD-SCHEME fingerprints with no current finding — these verdicts would be dropped by an apply. Stores already at the live scheme never orphan (see stale_*).

orphaned_sample.description: probe only: bounded sorted sample of the orphaned fingerprints (counts are authoritative; an apply records the full list verbatim in the migration journal).

per_store.description: probe only: open map of store file name (e.g. 'baseline.yaml') -> count of its stored fingerprints with no current finding. Only stores with orphans appear.

prescheme.description: probe only: true when a live store predates the fingerprint-scheme stamp, so orphans MAY be a fingerprint-formula change rather than source churn.

restored.description: rollback only: store file names restored from the pre-migration snapshot.

scanned_findings.description: probe only: number of findings the suppression-free scan produced (each carries an old/new fingerprint pair).

snapshot_prescheme.description: apply/resume: true when the snapshotted stores carried no scheme stamp (pre-scheme), so orphans may be a formula change — surfaced as a caution.

stale_cause.description: probe only: fixed explanation string for why a current-scheme entry can be stale.

stale_count.description: probe only: number of CURRENT-scheme store entries matching no current finding — baseline drift, NOT migration orphans; a rekey would not touch them and they do not dirty the probe.

stale_sample.description: probe only: bounded sorted sample of the stale fingerprints.

active_defect_entry.description: One active defect, with explain availability and suggested next tool calls; explanation is inlined only under scan(explain:true) for findings with a qualname, up to the cap.

reason.description: Why explain is unavailable (no qualname), or null.

explanation.description: Inlined taint provenance (same shape as the explanation slice of explain_taint).

source_resolution.description: C-10(c) honesty block: explicit resolved/unresolved verdict on the taint source, with reason + missing capability + enablement when unresolved.

tier_in.description: Actual (untrusted) tier arriving at the sink.

tier_out.description: Tier the sink declares it returns.

filigree_destination.description: Where findings were (or would be) sent, so a wrong-project write is visible.

project.description: The project pinned in the URL, or null when Filigree resolves it server-side.

filigree_emit_failures.description: PDR-0023 honesty surface: one record per finding that did NOT land in Filigree, so a PARTIAL ingest ('M of N emitted, K rejected because R') is byte-distinguishable from a clean emit. Empty on a clean run — but earned from real per-finding records, not hardwired.

cause.description: weft-reason carrier `cause`: the why (Filigree's detail, else the domain reason). Always present on a failure.

fingerprint.description: Wardline join key for the failed finding (absent when chunk-wide).

fix.description: weft-reason carrier `fix` (MANDATORY on a non-clean carrier): the remedy.

reason.description: Machine-readable failure case: rejected (Filigree refused this finding), validation_error (malformed body), scheme_mismatch (fingerprint-scheme drift — a join-miss, not a true-negative), partial (whole chunk rejected at the protocol layer; cause is the request, not the body).

reason_class.description: weft-reason (G1): the canonical reason_class this failure maps to (one of the closed 11 in contracts/weft-reason-vocab.json). validation_error maps to rejected; the domain term stays in `reason`/`cause`.

filigree_emit_status.description: Normalized Filigree emit status (always an object; configured:false when no emitter).

disabled_reason.description: Actionable reason (auth-rejected vs server error vs unreachable vs not configured), or null when reached.

failed.description: Count of un-ingested findings (derived from `failures`); 0 is earned, not assumed.

status.description: HTTP error status for soft failures; absent when not configured.

finding_entry.description: One finding (suppressed / engine-fact / informational display arrays).

loomweave_write_status.description: Normalized Loomweave taint-fact write status (always an object; configured:false when no client).

scan.description: Success payload of the wardline MCP `scan` tool (the dict _scan returns, served verbatim as structuredContent).

agent_summary.description: The stable agent-oriented handoff block (schema wardline-agent-summary-1): active defects first, suppressed debt visible, integration status explicit, suggested next tool calls.

active_defects.description: Non-suppressed defects in the displayed page (severity-sorted). Each entry carries explain/next_tool_calls hints; with explain:true an inlined explanation (capped — see truncation.explanations_truncated).

engine_facts.description: Engine diagnostic facts (WLN-ENGINE-*) in the displayed page.

gate.description: Gate echo inside the agent summary (no sub-gate attribution flags here; see the top-level gate block for those).

informational.description: Non-defect, non-engine-fact findings (metrics, classifications, suggestions) in the displayed page.

next_actions.description: Gate-aware suggested next tool calls, driven by the whole-project active count (not the displayed slice).

summary.description: Whole-project counts (never affected by where/pagination filters).

engine_facts.description: Kind.FACT findings with a WLN-ENGINE-* rule_id.

informational.description: ALL non-defect findings (engine facts included; the display array below excludes them).

suppressed_findings.description: Suppressed (baselined/waived/judged) defects in the displayed page.

truncation.description: Single pagination descriptor for the four display arrays (one ordered union: active, suppressed, engine facts, informational).

explanations_truncated.description: True when explain:true hit the inlining cap before covering every shown active defect.

findings_total.description: Size of the displayed union before paging.

max_findings.description: Effective page size; null means uncapped (full:true).

next_offset.description: Pass as offset to fetch the next page; null when complete.

files_scanned.description: Number of files discovered (see scope.files_analyzed for the delta-mode analyzed count).

filigree.description: Raw Filigree emit result; null when no emitter is configured.

auth_rejected.description: True when the emit was refused with 401/403.

failed.description: Count of un-ingested findings (derived from `failures`); 0 is earned from real records, not assumed.

status.description: HTTP error status (401/403/5xx) for soft failures; null on success or transport failure.

token_sent.description: Whether a bearer token was actually sent (splits a 401 into absent vs rejected).

gate.description: The pass/fail gate decision (a trip is data, not an error).

evaluated.description: Which population the gate judged (unsuppressed default vs suppression-honoring).

fail_on.description: The severity threshold evaluated, or null when no --fail-on ran.

fail_on_unanalyzed.description: Whether the unanalyzed sub-gate knob was set.

reason.description: Human-readable verdict naming the count/class of defects that decided it.

severity_tripped.description: Sub-gate attribution: the severity threshold tripped.

unanalyzed_tripped.description: Sub-gate attribution: the unanalyzed gate tripped.

verdict.description: NOT_EVALUATED when no authoritative severity gate judged the full gate population; FAILED iff tripped.

would_trip_at.description: Highest severity at which the gate WOULD trip on the evaluated population, or null if nothing would.

legis_artifact.description: OPTIONAL: the verbatim-postable signed scan object for legis POST /wardline/scan-results. Present only when a WARDLINE_LEGIS_ARTIFACT_KEY is provisioned or legis_artifact:true was passed, AND building it did not fail (a signing refusal omits it). Suppressed under summary_only:true unless legis_artifact:true is passed explicitly — summary_only promises the smallest gate payload.

artifact_signature.description: hmac-sha256:v2:<hex> over the canonical scan-minus-signature; present only on the signed (key + clean tree) path.

commit_sha.description: Present when provenance was readable (always on the signed path).

dirty.description: Present (true) only when the working tree was dirty (unsigned dev artifact).

findings.description: The gate population projected onto legis's accepted vocabulary.

properties.description: Trust-tier-valued properties only (plus suppression_reason proof on non-active defects); diagnostics dropped.

scan_scope.description: Signed scope binding: scan root, configured/resolved source roots, and realized files.

is_git_root.description: True only when the scan root is the containing git repository root.

resolved_source_roots.description: Configured source roots resolved relative to the signed scope base.

scan_root.description: Scan root relative to the git repository root when in git; otherwise '.'.

scanned_paths.description: Files actually discovered and analyzed, relative to the scan root.

tree_sha.description: Committed tree SHA; present on the signed path and best-effort otherwise.

legis_artifact_status.description: OPTIONAL: signed/dirty status of the legis artifact attempt. Present whenever the legis block was activated (key provisioned or legis_artifact:true), including when signing was refused and legis_artifact itself is absent.

dirty.description: Present only when the artifact was actually built (absent on a build refusal).

key_id.description: Non-secret short id of the HMAC key (first 8 hex of sha256), or null when unkeyed.

reason.description: Refusal/unverified reason (e.g. dirty-tree refusal), or null.

loomweave.description: Raw Loomweave taint-fact write result; null when no Loomweave client is configured.

scope.description: OPTIONAL: the delta-scan (--affected) honesty/provenance block. Present only when an `affected` scope was supplied; absent on a full scan. Declares whether the run analyzed a scoped subset (mode='delta', gate_authority='advisory') or fell back to a full scan (mode='full-fallback', gate_authority='gate-of-record'). In delta mode, only scoped files were analyzed; a clean delta is advisory and not a gate-of-record pass.

boundary_caveat.description: The fixed honesty caveat naming the in-scope-correctness hazard (cross-file taint outside the analyzed set is not computed).

entities_requested.description: Number of input items in the supplied affected scope.

fell_back_count.description: Entities resolved via the spoofable qualname-locator path rather than an authoritative SEI.

files_analyzed.description: Files actually analyzed; the scoped subset in delta mode, == files_discovered in full-fallback.

gate_authority.description: Machine-readable companion to mode: advisory in delta mode (a clean delta is not a full-tree pass), gate-of-record in full-fallback.

in_scope_findings.description: Displayed (post-filter) finding count for the affected entities.

loomweave_used.description: Whether the authoritative SEI (loomweave) resolution path fired for any entity.

mode.description: delta = a scoped file subset was analyzed; full-fallback = the scope resolved zero files (empty / all-unresolvable) so a full scan ran (fail-closed honesty).

stale_sei_count.description: Entities whose SEI resolved to a now-absent qualname (loomweave stale vs working tree).

unresolved_entities.description: Entities that did not resolve to any file even in delta mode (scanned subset, not fallback).

summary.description: Whole-project finding counts. active+baselined+waived+judged+informational == total; unanalyzed is an overlay, not a partition member.

unanalyzed.description: Files discovered but never analysed (parse errors / too-deep / missing source roots); overlay count.

scan_file_findings.description: Success payload of the scan_file_findings tool: one-shot scan -> (optionally) emit findings to Filigree -> (optionally) promote selected active defects into tracked issues.

active_defects.description: Every active (non-suppressed) defect in the scan, each with its per-finding promotion and identity-attach outcome.

explanation.description: One-hop taint provenance slice. Present only when the finding has a qualname AND the scan produced an analysis context.

immediate_tainted_callee.description: The directly-called function that contributed the taint, if resolved.

resolved_call_count.description: Calls inside the function the analyzer resolved.

source_boundary_qualname.description: Qualname of the boundary function the taint originated from (one hop only).

tier_in.description: Actual (untrusted) trust tier arriving at the sink.

tier_out.description: Trust tier the sink declares it returns.

unresolved_call_count.description: Calls the analyzer could not resolve.

fingerprint.description: Stable finding fingerprint (the promotion join key).

identity_attach.description: Outcome of binding the finding's Loomweave entity identity to the promoted issue.

attached.description: Whether the entity association was successfully attached.

attempted.description: Whether an identity attach was attempted at all.

binding_kind.description: Whether the binding used a rename-stable SEI or a legacy locator; null when no binding was attempted.

content_hash.description: Entity content hash captured at attach time; null when unresolved.

entity_id.description: The entity identifier used — a 'loomweave:eid:...' SEI or a legacy locator.

reason.description: Why the attach was not attempted or was skipped; null on success.

path.description: Repo-relative file path of the finding.

promotion.description: Per-finding Filigree promote outcome for this defect.

attempted.description: Whether a promote was actually attempted (false in dry_run, when unselected, or when no filer is configured).

created.description: True when the promote created a NEW issue.

disabled_reason.description: Why the promote did not happen or failed soft; null on success.

issue_id.description: The Filigree issue id; null when not attempted, unreachable, or not found.

not_found.description: True when Filigree was reachable but the fingerprint is unknown to it (404).

reachable.description: Whether Filigree was reachable for the promote; null when not attempted.

selected.description: Whether this finding was in the selection set.

qualname.description: Dotted module-qualified name of the enclosing callable; null when the finding has no callable anchor.

rule_id.description: Rule that produced the finding (e.g. PY-WL-101).

files_scanned.description: Number of files the scan analyzed.

filigree_emit.description: Outcome of bulk-emitting scan findings to Filigree (runs only when findings were selected and an emitter is configured).

configured.description: Whether a Filigree emitter is configured for this server.

disabled_reason.description: Why the emit failed soft — the discriminated 401/403-vs-5xx-vs-transport ladder ('not configured', 'filigree rejected the token (401)...', 'filigree unreachable'). null means success OR no emit was attempted (dry-run / nothing selected) — read `reachable` to tell them apart (null = no attempt).

failed.description: Count of findings that did NOT land in Filigree (derived from `failures`). 0 here is earned from real per-finding records, not assumed — see `failures` for which and why.

failures.description: PDR-0023 honesty surface: one record per finding that failed to land, so a PARTIAL ingest ('M of N emitted, K rejected because R') is distinguishable from a clean emit ('all N emitted'). Empty on a clean run — but earned, not hardwired.

cause.description: weft-reason carrier `cause`: the why (Filigree's detail, else the domain reason). Always present on a failure (a failure is never clean).

fingerprint.description: The wardline join key for the failed finding (absent when the failure is chunk-wide and not attributable to one finding).

fix.description: weft-reason carrier `fix` (MANDATORY on a non-clean carrier): the remedial action.

reason.description: Machine-readable failure case: rejected (Filigree refused this finding), validation_error (malformed body), scheme_mismatch (fingerprint-scheme drift — a join-miss, not a true-negative), partial (the whole chunk was rejected at the protocol layer, so the cause is the request not the body).

reason_class.description: weft-reason (G1): the canonical reason_class this failure maps to (one of the closed 11 in contracts/weft-reason-vocab.json). validation_error maps to rejected; the domain term stays in `reason`/`cause`.

reachable.description: Whether Filigree was reachable for the emit; null when no emit was attempted.

gate.description: The pass/fail gate decision for this scan (a trip is data, not an error).

exit_class.description: CLI-equivalent exit class: 0 clean, 1 gate tripped (2 is reserved for tool errors and never appears here).

fail_on.description: The severity threshold the gate evaluated (e.g. 'ERROR'); null when no threshold was given.

verdict.description: NOT_EVALUATED = no authoritative severity threshold judged the full gate population; PASSED/FAILED = configured gate(s) judged authoritatively. Never reads a bare scan or advisory delta as a clean pass.

would_trip_at.description: Highest severity at which the gate WOULD trip on the evaluated population; null when nothing would trip.

mode.description: Selection mode that ran: dry_run (nothing promoted), all_active (every active defect selected), or fingerprints (explicit selection).

selected_count.description: How many selected fingerprints matched known active defects.

summary.description: Finding counts by suppression class for the whole scan.

judged.description: Defects suppressed by judge FALSE_POSITIVE records.

unanalyzed.description: Files discovered but never analyzed (benign no-module skips excluded).

unknown_fingerprints.description: Explicitly-requested fingerprints that are not among the scan's active defects.

scan_job_cancel.description: File-backed scan-job status. Non-terminal jobs include heartbeat/pid/progress; terminal jobs include summary/gate/artifacts when the worker reached them.

scan_job_start.description: File-backed scan-job status. Non-terminal jobs include heartbeat/pid/progress; terminal jobs include summary/gate/artifacts when the worker reached them.

scan_job_status.description: File-backed scan-job status. Non-terminal jobs include heartbeat/pid/progress; terminal jobs include summary/gate/artifacts when the worker reached them.

verify_attestation.description: Result of verifying an attestation bundle: signature check (always, offline) plus optional reproducibility re-derivation against the current tree.

mismatches.description: Top-level payload keys that differ between the recorded and re-derived payloads. Empty unless reproduced is false.

note.description: Fixed caveat: reproducibility holds against the RECORDED commit; a mismatch may mean the tree moved, not tamper.

reproduced.description: null when reproduce was not requested (or no root); true when the re-derived payload's canonical bytes equal the recorded payload's; false otherwise. A false may mean the tree moved on, not tamper.

signature_valid.description: True iff the recomputed HMAC over the recorded payload matches the stored signature (schema tag, alg, and key_id must all match too).

waiver_add.description: Success payload of the wardline MCP `waiver_add` tool: the waiver that now covers the fingerprint (newly added, or the pre-existing one when a waiver for the fingerprint was already present). When an inline entity_symbol was supplied but did NOT resolve to a SEI, the waiver is NOT written and only {fingerprint, created:false, unresolved_input} is returned (never an unbound-but-looks-bound record).

already_exists.description: True when a waiver for this fingerprint already existed and the returned fields describe that existing waiver; false when this call added a new waiver. Absent on an unresolved_input refusal.

binding_kind.description: Whether the stored binding is a rename-stable SEI or a legacy locator; null when no entity binding was supplied.

created.description: Present only on an unresolved_input refusal: always false (the waiver was NOT written).

entity_locator.description: Human-readable locator for the bound entity (e.g. 'python:function:pkg.mod.fn'); null when no inline entity binding was supplied.

entity_sei.description: The rename-surviving Loomweave SEI bound to this waiver's code entity, or the opaque entity_id supplied verbatim; null when no inline entity binding was supplied.

expires.description: Waiver expiry as an ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD). Null only when a pre-existing waiver loaded from the waivers file carries no expiry (the tool itself requires expires on new waivers). Absent on an unresolved_input refusal.

fingerprint.description: The finding fingerprint the waiver covers.

reason.description: The waiver's reason. When already_exists=true this is the EXISTING waiver's reason, which may differ from the one passed in this call. Absent on an unresolved_input refusal (nothing was written).

unresolved_input.description: Present only when an inline entity_symbol was supplied but did not resolve to a SEI. The waiver was NOT written. Canonical weft-reason carrier.

cause.description: Why the supplied entity reference did not resolve.

fix.description: The actionable next step to make it resolve.
